description: Comprehensive question type definitions for interactive coding assessment platform

question_types.description: Standard multiple-choice question with exactly one correct answer from 4 options

example.questionStem: What is the output of the following code?
example.explanation: The String "Java" has 4 characters, so length() returns 4.

question_types.description: Question with two or more correct answers from multiple options. User must select all correct options.

example.questionStem: Which of the following are functional interfaces in Java?
example.explanation: Functional interfaces have exactly one abstract method. Runnable has run(), Comparator has compare(). List and ArrayList have multiple abstract methods.

question_types.description: Arrange items in correct sequential order by dragging and dropping. Tests understanding of processes, algorithms, and workflows.

example.questionStem: Arrange the Spring Boot startup flow in correct order:

question_types.description: Connect concepts on the left to their definitions/descriptions on the right by drawing lines or drag-matching.

example.questionStem: Match each Java collection to its characteristic:

question_types.description: Match multiple code snippets to their corresponding outputs. Tests code tracing ability.

example.questionStem: Match each code snippet to its output:

question_types.description: Given outputs, identify which code snippet produces each output. Reverse of code-to-output matching.

example.questionStem: Match each output to the code that produces it:

question_types.description: Complete a code statement or concept by filling in one or more blanks with the correct text.

example.questionStem: Fill in the blank to create an ArrayList of Strings:

question_types.description: A code snippet with a missing method call or logic block. User types the correct code to complete functionality.

example.questionStem: Complete the stream pipeline to double each number and print it:

question_types.description: Rearrange shuffled code lines/blocks into the correct order to form a valid program.

distractors.description: Extra incorrect lines that should not be used

example.questionStem: Arrange the code blocks to form a valid Java program that prints 'Hello':

question_types.description: Read a code snippet and predict what it will print to the console. Tests code tracing and mental execution.

example.questionStem: What will be printed to the console?

ui_recommendations.placeholder_text: Enter each output on a new line

question_types.description: Identify bugs, runtime errors, or logical errors in the given code. May require fixing the code or identifying the exception.

example.questionStem: What error will occur when this code runs?

question_types.description: Identify all compilation/syntax errors in a code snippet. Tests language syntax knowledge.

example.questionStem: Identify the compilation error in this code:

errors.errorDescription: Cannot use primitive type 'int' as generic type parameter

question_types.description: Identify code quality issues, anti-patterns, or design violations in a code snippet.

example.questionStem: Identify the code smell and suggest improvement:

smells.description: Excessive null checking with deep nesting
smells.refactoring_suggestion: Use Optional or early return pattern: if(user == null || user.getName() == null || user.getName().isEmpty()) return;

question_types.description: Review a pull request snippet and identify issues related to performance, security, design, or correctness.

context.description: Description of what the PR is trying to accomplish

example.questionStem: Review this code and identify potential issues:
example.context: API endpoint for fetching user profile data

issues.description: SQL Injection vulnerability - user input directly concatenated into query
issues.suggestion: Use parameterized query: SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?

question_types.description: Drag and drop SQL clauses to construct a valid query. Tests SQL composition knowledge.

example.questionStem: Build a SQL query to find all employees with salary > 50000 ordered by name:
example.targetDescription: Select name and salary from employees where salary exceeds 50000, sorted alphabetically

question_types.description: Given a table schema with sample data and a SQL query, predict the output result set.

example.questionStem: What will this query return?
example.query: SELECT Name FROM employee WHERE id > 1;

question_types.description: Design or choose the correct REST API endpoint for a given scenario. Tests RESTful convention knowledge.

example.questionStem: Which is the correct REST endpoint to fetch a specific customer's details?
example.scenario: Design a REST API to fetch customer details by their ID

question_types.description: Identify and match architecture components to their correct layers or roles in a system diagram.

example.questionStem: Drag each component to its correct architectural layer:

question_types.description: Organize system components into the correct architecture by dragging cards into positions or groups.

example.questionStem: Design an e-commerce system by arranging components and drawing connections:
example.scenario: Build a microservice architecture for an online shopping platform

question_types.description: Arrange or complete message flows in a sequence diagram showing component interactions.

missingMessages.description: Messages the user needs to place

example.questionStem: Complete the sequence diagram for a login flow:

question_types.description: Answer questions based on a flowchart diagram, or complete missing parts of a flowchart.

example.questionStem: Based on the flowchart, what happens when the condition (i <= 10) is FALSE?

question_types.description: Full code editor where users write, compile, and run code against test cases. Real-time execution environment.

example.questionStem: Two Sum
example.problemDescription: Given an array of integers and a target, return indices of two numbers that add up to target.

question_types.description: Write unit test cases for given code to achieve coverage or catch bugs.

example.questionStem: Write JUnit 5 tests for this calculator method to achieve >90% coverage:

question_types.description: Questions about testing concepts, mocking, assertions, and test design patterns.

example.questionStem: Which assertion verifies that a method throws an exception?

question_types.description: Refactor given code to improve quality while maintaining behavior. Validates both correctness and improvement.

testCases.description: Tests that must still pass after refactoring

example.questionStem: Refactor this code to reduce nesting and improve readability:

question_types.description: Optimize code for better time/space complexity or runtime performance.

example.questionStem: Optimize this function from O(n²) to O(n):

question_types.description: Identify security vulnerabilities and write secure code. Based on OWASP Top 10.

example.questionStem: Identify the security vulnerability and provide a fix:

vulnerabilities.description: User input directly rendered in HTML without sanitization
vulnerabilities.fix: Use HTML encoding: HtmlUtils.htmlEscape(query) or use template engine with auto-escaping

question_types.description: Complex scenarios mimicking real-world development problems requiring multiple skill application.

example.questionStem: Production Incident Response
example.scenario: Your e-commerce platform is experiencing 500 errors during checkout. Logs show database connection pool exhaustion. Walk through the diagnosis and resolution.

steps.description: Identify the root cause from the error logs
Choose the correct monitoring metric to watch

question_types.description: Multi-part assessment based on a detailed case study. Tests holistic problem-solving across multiple dimensions.

example.title: Design a Scalable Notification System
example.caseStudy: TechCorp needs a notification system handling 1M users, supporting email, SMS, push, and in-app notifications with preferences and rate limiting.

question_types.description: Build, identify, or fix microservice architectures by dragging components and defining communication patterns.

example.questionStem: Identify and label the components in this microservice architecture:
example.scenario: An e-commerce platform with service discovery, API gateway, and event-driven order processing

question_types.description: Design database schemas, normalize tables, or identify issues in existing schemas.

example.questionStem: Design a database schema for a quiz platform with users, quizzes, and results:
requirements: Users can create quizzes with multiple questions
Questions have 4 options with one correct answer

question_types.description: Diagnose network issues using simulated tools (ping, traceroute, netstat, etc.).

example.questionStem: Users report they cannot access the internal API. Diagnose the issue.
example.scenario: A microservice cannot reach the database server. Use the available tools to identify the problem.

question_types.description: Arrange CI/CD pipeline stages in correct order or fix pipeline configurations.

example.questionStem: Arrange the CI/CD pipeline stages in correct order:

question_types.description: Design AWS cloud architectures by selecting and connecting services for given requirements.

example.questionStem: Design a serverless API architecture for a mobile app backend:

question_types.description: Build a functional pipeline by dragging operators in sequence. Tests functional programming and data flow understanding.

example.questionStem: Build a Java Stream pipeline to filter even numbers, double them, and collect to a list:

xp_system.description: Experience points earned per question based on difficulty and speed

categories.name: Topic Master
categories.description: Answer 50+ questions correctly in a topic

categories.name: First Blood
categories.description: First to answer correctly in battle mode

daily_challenges.description: One challenge per day with bonus XP

modes.name: Speed Round
modes.description: Answer as many questions as possible in 5 minutes

modes.name: Countdown Challenge
modes.description: Each question has decreasing time limit

modes.name: Time Attack
modes.description: Fixed time per question, wrong answer = time penalty

adaptive_difficulty.description: Adjusts question difficulty based on user performance using ELO rating system

adjustment_rules: 3 correct in a row → increase difficulty
2 wrong in a row → decrease difficulty
Time-based: fast correct = larger rating increase

question_selection.strategy: Select questions within ±200 of user's current rating
question_selection.weakness_targeting: 20% of questions target user's weakest topics

levels.name: Direction
levels.description: Points to the relevant concept or API

generation.method: AI-powered contextual hints based on question content and user's common mistakes
generation.personalization: Hints tailored to user's skill gaps

ai_hints.cooldown: One hint per 30 seconds to encourage independent thinking

mechanisms.description: Multi-step problems score each step independently
For numeric or code answers, partial credit for close answers
For matching/sorting, credit per correct placement
For coding problems, credit per passing test case
Score decreases slightly with time spent

evaluation_engine.description: Backend evaluation configuration for all question types

api_schema.description: Backend API endpoints for question management
